paper_title: Effect of associated bacteria on gnotobiotic Artemia performance

entities.description: The primary author of the research paper.

entities.id: College of Aquaculture and Fisheries (CAF), Can Tho University
entities.description: The academic institution where the research was conducted, located in Vietnam.
The primary field of study regarding the farming of aquatic organisms.
The study of Artemia growth and survival in controlled, bacteria-free or known-bacteria environments.
The process where bacteria improve water quality by removing toxic metabolites.
A controlled laboratory culture system used to evaluate the effect of specific bacteria.
Bacteria-free baker’s yeast used as the main food source for Artemia.
Nine unidentified bacteria strains isolated from culture water used as supplements.
The process of removing Artemia cyst shells using sodium hypochlorite.
One-way analysis of variance used for statistical comparison of datasets.
Numerical data on survival and individual length checked for homogeneity and normal distribution.
The range of survival rates observed in Artemia across different treatments.
The specific density at which bacteria were added to the culture.
The measurement of the physical growth of the Artemia nauplii.
The concentration of salt in the artificial seawater used for the culture.
The controlled thermal environment for the experimental tubes.
